Inputs • Username / Email • Password Processing • Validate credentials against stored records • Generate authentication token Outputs • Success: Authentication token returned • Failure: Unauthorized response Validation Rules • Credentials must be valid • Invalid login attempts must be rejected FR-02: Token Validation Description The system must validate authentication tokens for all protected API requests. Inputs • JWT token Processing • Verify token signature • Check token expiration • Extract user identity Outputs • Success: Request proceeds
--- PAGE 31 ---
• Failure: Unauthorized response Validation Rules • Token must not be expired • Token must be valid and untampered 3.4 Customer Management FR-03: Create Customer Description The system must allow creation of a new customer record. Inputs • Customer Name • Contact Details Processing • Validate input data • Store customer record Outputs • Success: Customer created with ID • Failure: Validation error Validation Rules • Name is mandatory • Duplicate entries must be prevented (if applicable) FR-04: Update Customer Description The system must allow updating existing customer details.
--- PAGE 32 ---

--- PAGE 83 ---
The same type of business outcome shall not return conflicting status code behavior across different modules without a justified reason. 8.9 Authentication Interface Requirements 8.9.1 Authentication Endpoint Behavior The API shall provide a secure mechanism for obtaining an authentication token or equivalent authorized session artifact. Requirements • authentication requests shall validate submitted credentials • successful authentication shall return a token or equivalent access artifact • failed authentication shall return a clear but secure rejection response • authentication failures shall not leak sensitive security details 8.9.2 Authenticated Request Behavior Protected endpoints shall require the caller to present valid authentication credentials with the request. Requirements • missing credentials shall result in request rejection • invalid credentials shall result in request rejection • expired credentials shall result in request rejection • authorized requests shall proceed only after successful validation 8.10 Authorization Interface Requirements Where role-based or permission-based access rules apply, the API shall enforce them consistently. Requirements

--- PAGE 40 ---
4.4 Authentication and Access Rules BRULE-05: Authenticated Access Required All protected API operations must require successful authentication. Validation Requirement • Requests without valid credentials or token must be rejected. BRULE-06: Token Validity The system must reject expired, malformed, tampered, or otherwise invalid authentication tokens. Validation Requirement • Token structure, expiration, and signature must be validated before request processing. BRULE-07: Identity Context Every authorized transaction must be processed within the context of the authenticated user identity. Explanation: Where required, the system must associate changes or actions with the authenticated user. 4.5 Master Data Rules BRULE-08: Customer Record Completeness A customer record must contain all mandatory attributes required to identify and manage the customer in operational and financial processes. Validation Requirements • Customer name must be provided. • Required business contact fields must not be blank if marked mandatory by the domain model.
--- PAGE 41 ---

• response fields shall be documented • authentication requirements shall be documented • expected status codes and common error conditions shall be documented • API documentation should be available in a format suitable for developer consumption 8.18 Interface Logging and Traceability Requirements Requirements • requests to business-critical endpoints should be traceable for support and audit purposes • failures in interface processing should be logged consistently • financial and security-sensitive operations should have stronger trace visibility • request correlation support should be considered for troubleshooting across services or layered components 8.19 API Acceptance Criteria The API and interface requirements are considered satisfied when: • the API exposes clearly structured and consistently named business endpoints • request and response behavior is predictable across modules • authentication and authorization are enforced correctly • validation failures and business rule violations return controlled, meaningful responses • status code usage is consistent with business outcomes • list and report endpoints support practical retrieval behavior • financial operations apply stronger controls and clearer traceability • the interface can be understood and consumed reliably by approved client systems
